dag_desc: Roadmap engine repo · r2 · public-release prep for ML Prague 2026 (poster ~2026-05-07). The repo is currently a private GitHub mirror of one solo developer's dogfooding; flipping it public exposes ~500 LOC of theater-tier defensive wrappers, 350K+ of internal execution state (timestamps, sibling-project references, handoff notes), the maintainer's full Claude Code skill template library (mostly tied to other-project doctrine), hardcoded-path mirror-sync hooks, a commit-msg hook that walls off contributors, and an `npm install roadmap` README instruction that points at someone else's package. The strategic shape is two-phase: pre-conference is ship-shape minimum (scrub + license + repo metadata + minimum contributor surface + verify), post-conference (~2 weeks later) is full contribution surface (CONTRIBUTING.md, Discussions, PR acceptance). Phase 2 is plan-mode because its concrete shape depends on conference feedback that does not yet exist. The terminal flips repo visibility to public and tags v0.2.0. Anti-pattern guard: this round contains zero observation nodes; surfaces were inventoried in conversation with the maintainer during spec authoring, findings embedded here. The literal blocklist of sibling-project codenames + reserved paths lives in a gitignored sidecar (.roadmap/leak-patterns.local.json) so the spec itself is publishable. CLUSTERS · STRIP (rip cruft) · CURATE (clean remaining content) · CONTRIB-MIN (minimum contributor surface for conference burst) · GH-SETTINGS (repo metadata + branch protection) · CI (replace husky's gates with workflows) · VERIFY (pre-flip integration gates) · PUBLIC (flip + tag). NO BATCH PARTITIONING · clusters group by concern · depends:[] is the only sequencing.

npm_target.rationale: unscoped 'roadmap' is already published by another author; scope is verified available (404 on registry probe 2026-04-30)

rollout_stance.phase_1: ship public · issues ON · PRs technically allowed but README + PR template say 'deferred until ~2026-05-15' · Discussions OFF
rollout_stance.phase_2: post-conference · open Discussions · publish CONTRIBUTING.md · accept PRs · npm publish
rollout_stance.phase_2_trigger: conference + ~1 week of issue triage informs concrete shape

leak_patterns_source.rationale: literal patterns (sibling-project codenames, reserved paths) must not appear in any committed file — including this spec. validators read the sidecar at runtime; if missing, the validator fails closed. publish the spec without ever embedding the patterns it enforces.

dev_hook_relocation.activation: git config core.hooksPath scripts/dev-hooks/ (manual, documented in scripts/dev-hooks/README.md)
dev_hook_relocation.rationale: public users get no surprise hooks on `pnpm install`; Griffin re-enables locally via one command

metadata.autonomy_policy: execute scrub + curate + ci nodes autonomously; pause for human approval before term-flip-public-and-tag (visibility change is irreversible without coordination)

tasks.desc: Round-2 entry. Confirm session state: feat/r2-public-release-prep checked out, CLAUDE.md sha matches inputs[], no in-flight uncommitted work. The receipt names the operator and time and pins the starting head. SCENARIO · given main is at fc1e902 and this branch is created from main · when init runs · then the round is open and downstream nodes can begin.

Author scripts/leak-scan.js — a small Node module that reads .roadmap/leak-patterns.local.json and scans tracked files for unallowlisted hits. Interface: `node scripts/leak-scan.js [path...]` (default: `git ls-files` output). Exit 0 on clean, exit 1 with `LEAK: <file>:<line> :: <pattern>` lines on dirty. Honors the per-path allowlist (e.g. allow 'fleet' literal in tests/fleet-*.test.ts but not anywhere else). Fails closed if the sidecar is missing. The scanner is the single source of leak-policy across all downstream validators (c-scrub-*, s-delete-internal-docs, c-curate-roadmap-example, v-leak-sweep) — no validator embeds patterns inline. SCENARIO · given the maintainer runs `node scripts/leak-scan.js` after a content edit · when the file is clean · then exit 0; when it has an unallowlisted hit · then file:line printed and exit 1.

validate.command: test -x scripts/leak-scan.js || node scripts/leak-scan.js --help >/dev/null 2>&1 || node scripts/leak-scan.js README.md

tasks.desc: Delete the `roadmap spec` subcommand wholesale. SPEC-TIME FINDING: `roadmap spec` exposes plan/select/status sub-subcommands tied to a 'plan gallery' feature that depends on the maintainer's specific spec-authoring workflow; it is undocumented in README and adds 309 LOC to the public CLI surface. Concrete: `git rm src/cli/spec.ts`; remove the dispatch case in src/cli.ts; remove the `spec <sub>` line from the help text in src/cli/help.ts. If src/cli/commands/ has spec-related files, delete those too. Sweep references in tests/ — delete or update tests that exercise spec subcommand. SCENARIO · given a public reader runs `roadmap help` · when they read the command list · then `spec` does not appear; AND `roadmap spec` returns an unknown-command error. Validator: file gone, help text clean, tests pass.

Replace `roadmap init` CLI behavior with LLM-runnable prose. RATIONALE: user environments vary too widely for a rigid script — package manager (npm/pnpm/yarn/bun), CLAUDE.md anchor conventions, skills-directory location, whether they use Claude Code at all, monorepo vs solo-repo, .gitignore conventions, etc. A markdown setup doc that an LLM agent (or careful human) reads and adapts to context handles the variance better than a CLI that tries to enumerate cases. CONCRETE: (1) Author docs/SETUP.md as LLM-runnable prose: 'paste this into Claude Code or another agent in your target repo: <setup prompt>'. The prompt explains the desired end state (CLAUDE.md fragment present + .roadmap/ initialized + a starter spec ready) and lets the agent adapt to the user's setup. (2) Strip src/cli/init.ts down to a thin stub that prints docs/SETUP.md to stdout (or just prints 'See https://github.com/Ocean-Synaptics/roadmap/blob/main/docs/SETUP.md and paste the embedded prompt into your agent') — keeps the discoverability of `roadmap init` as a command without pretending it can autosetup. (3) Drop installSkills() and installFragment() helpers since they don't run anymore. templates/claude-md-fragment.md stays as raw material for the doc. (4) Update README's quick-start to call out `roadmap init` as 'prints setup instructions' and link to the doc. SCENARIO · given a user with a non-standard repo setup · when they run `roadmap init` · then they get a paste-friendly prompt to feed their LLM, which then handles their specific environment. Validator: docs/SETUP.md exists with paste-prompt content; init.ts is small (<60 LOC); no auto-write to ~/.claude/.

Collapse the v0.2.0 public package surface to a single entry. CURRENT STATE: package.json `exports` exposes 6 entry points (., ./protocol, ./recovery, ./validation, ./versioning, ./agent) corresponding to src/index*.ts files. MOST are split-points the maintainer uses for tree-shaking; few documented externally. CHANGE: keep `.` (main) only; the others become internal — accessible via deep imports if anyone insists, but not part of the documented public surface. The split-point .ts files stay in src/ (don't refactor); just drop them from `exports` map. PHASE 2 may re-expose specific ones if external demand arises. SCENARIO · given a contributor reads package.json to understand the public API · when they look at `exports` · then they see one entry, not six. Validator: jq returns exactly one key under exports.

validate.command: jq -e '(.exports | type == "string") or (.exports | keys | length == 1)' package.json

tasks.desc: Delete the gitsafe pattern. Files removed: src/lib/gitsafe-loader.ts (203 LOC), src/lib/topology/enforcement-rules.ts (clone-role rules referencing ~ paths), .roadmap/enforcement.json, tests/gitsafe-multirepo.test.ts. Call sites: src/cli/make.ts (1 call to safeReadFile) and src/cli/shared.ts (safeReadFile + enforceMainBranch + getGitsafe + initGitsafe + denylist plumbing) — replace safeReadFile with readFileSync, drop enforceMainBranch from shared.ts exports + all callers, drop the gitsafe loader init from CLI entry. Topology module's surviving exports (if any are non-gitsafe) move into other lib/ files or are deleted with the module. SCENARIO · given a contributor is reading src/cli/shared.ts trying to understand why a wrapper around fs.readFileSync exists · when they grep for the threat model · then they find none and the wrapper itself is gone, not just commented out. Validators verify zero residual references in src/ + tests/.

validate.command: test ! -e src/lib/gitsafe-loader.ts && test ! -e src/lib/topology/enforcement-rules.ts && test ! -e .roadmap/enforcement.json && test ! -e tests/gitsafe-multirepo.test.ts

tasks.desc: Move active hooks to scripts/dev-hooks/ for Griffin's local dogfooding; remove husky from the public surface. Concrete moves: .husky/{pre-commit,commit-msg,prepare-commit-msg,post-commit,post-push} → scripts/dev-hooks/{pre-commit,commit-msg,prepare-commit-msg,post-commit,post-push}. Then `git rm -r .husky/`. Drop 'husky' from package.json devDependencies. Change package.json prepare script from 'pnpm run build && husky' to 'pnpm run build' (so a clone-and-install for a contributor never auto-installs hooks). Edit scripts/dev-hooks/post-push to read the dev-clone path from env (`ROADMAP_DEV_CLONE`) instead of hardcoded ~/src/.dev/roadmap; document. Edit scripts/dev-hooks/pre-commit to drop the gitsafe denylist gate (gitsafe is gone) and the DAG-origin gate; keep typecheck + DAG-define for main only. Write scripts/dev-hooks/README.md explaining: 'these are the maintainer's local hooks; activate with `git config core.hooksPath scripts/dev-hooks/`; not run on `pnpm install`.' SCENARIO · given a contributor clones the repo and runs `pnpm install && git commit` · when they commit · then no roadmap-specific hook fires and the commit lands; AND given Griffin runs the one-line activate command in his clone · when he commits · then his hooks fire as before. Validators check husky is gone from package.json + .husky removed + scripts/dev-hooks/ exists.

Extend .gitignore so future trail/handoff/completed emissions don't re-pollute the public tree. Add: dist/ (build output should never be committed), .roadmap/trail.jsonl, .roadmap/completed.json, .roadmap/.handoff/, .roadmap/round-*/, .roadmap/tasks/, .roadmap/heads/ (curate node will repopulate with one example), .boot/, .specify/, *.tsbuildinfo, .r1.5-init.marker, init.marker, BRANCH_INTENT_GAPS.txt, ROADMAP_TOPOLOGY.txt, .vitest-results.json. Keep .gitignore-existing entries intact. SCENARIO · given the maintainer runs `roadmap orient` and `roadmap advance` after this round · when receipts and trail entries get written · then `git status` shows them as ignored, not modified. Validator: every listed path appears in .gitignore.

validate.command: for p in 'dist/' '.roadmap/trail.jsonl' '.roadmap/completed.json' '.roadmap/.handoff/' '.roadmap/round-' '.roadmap/heads/' '*.tsbuildinfo' 'BRANCH_INTENT_GAPS.txt' 'ROADMAP_TOPOLOGY.txt' 'init.marker'; do grep -qF "$p" .gitignore || { echo missing: $p; exit 1; }; done

tasks.desc: Remove private notes and committed build output from the working tree. Concretely git rm: BRANCH_INTENT_GAPS.txt, init.marker, ROADMAP_TOPOLOGY.txt, viewer/.r1.5-init.marker, viewer/tsconfig.tsbuildinfo, governance/ (any tracked files), .boot/ (if tracked), dist/ (whole directory; rebuild from source). Sweep for other `*.marker` and `*GAPS*.txt` and `*TOPOLOGY*.txt`. Depends on s-gitignore-runtime-state so the patterns are already in place when files are removed (prevents re-add via subsequent build). SCENARIO · given a fresh clone of the repo · when a user runs `ls -la` · then they see no private notes, no committed build output, no leftover marker files. Validator: each deleted path no longer exists; dist/ rebuild produces same files but ignored.

validate.command: for p in BRANCH_INTENT_GAPS.txt init.marker ROADMAP_TOPOLOGY.txt viewer/.r1.5-init.marker viewer/tsconfig.tsbuildinfo; do test ! -e "$p" || { echo still present: $p; exit 1; }; done

tasks.desc: Delete templates/skills/ wholesale. AUDIT FINDING (spec-time): of the 21 skill template files, 16 reference sibling-project doctrine in their explanatory body text. Of the remaining 5 ('roadmap-*' family), only 2 (`roadmap-night.md`, `roadmap-term.md`) survive a leak-pattern grep clean; the other 3 (`roadmap-orient`, `roadmap-spec`, `roadmap-auto`) reference the maintainer's other-project rounds and §-anchors in their teaching examples. None of the templates are part of the protocol — they are maintainer-tooling for a separate `claude-code` agent stack, mirrored into this repo only because of a sync-feature in r1. They do not belong in v0.2.0. Concrete: `git rm -r templates/skills/`. Phase 2 may republish a generic skill set as a separate package. SCENARIO · given a public-repo browser opens the tracked tree · when they look for `templates/skills/` · then it is not there. Validator: directory does not exist.

Sweep docs/ + specs/ + playbooks/ for round-internal artifacts that describe completed maintainer work and don't contribute to public understanding. CANDIDATES TO DELETE (verify each at run time): any docs/*-verification.md, docs/review-response.md, docs/*-spec.md that documents a completed round's work for the maintainer's own audit trail. KEEP candidates: docs/Downs-CompilingAgentState-MLPrague2026.pdf (the conference paper) if present; any doc that documents a generic public concept (validator strength, receipt protocol, pattern enrichment if it names the protocol-level pattern not the round). For surviving .spec.json files in docs/, audit content for sidecar-blocklist hits; if clean, keep — they tell the protocol's evolution story. The specs/ directory (10 older roadmap rounds with generic names like chain-terminal, codebase-hygiene, etc.) — keep, they're project archeology. SCENARIO · given a contributor browses docs/ to understand the project · when they read · then every surviving doc is either reference material or a public-relevant spec. Validator: leak-sweep across these directories using the gitignored sidecar's blocklist returns zero unallowlisted hits.

validate.command: node -e "const p=JSON.parse(require('fs').readFileSync('.roadmap/leak-patterns.local.json','utf8'));const cp=require('child_process');const pats=[...p.blocked_anywhere,...p.blocked_paths].map(s=>s.replace(/[.*+?^${}()|[\\]\\\\]/g,'\\\\$&')).join('|');const out=cp.execSync('git ls-files docs/ specs/ playbooks/ 2>/dev/null',{encoding:'utf8'}).split('\\n').filter(Boolean);for(const f of out){try{const c=require('fs').readFileSync(f,'utf8');if(new RegExp(pats,'i').test(c)){console.error('LEAK:',f);process.exit(1)}}catch{}}"

tasks.desc: Replace the live dogfooding state in .roadmap/ with one curated example DAG that tells the project's own story without leaking timestamps, customer references, or internal handoffs. Concrete actions: (1) `git rm -r .roadmap/{trail.jsonl,completed.json,.handoff,round-1,round-1.5,tasks,heads}` — full reset since the gitignore from s-gitignore-runtime-state now covers them. (2) Author a small `.roadmap/heads/example-public-release.json` (or use a simplified version of the current head.json with customer refs scrubbed and node descs rewritten in generic prose). Aim for ~10-15 nodes, illustrates the produce/consume/depends shape. (3) Author a tiny `.roadmap/completed.json` showing 2-3 completion receipts in the canonical schema. (4) Write `.roadmap/README.md` (one screen) explaining: 'this directory shows roadmap dogfooding itself; the example DAG is illustrative; live runtime state is gitignored.' SCENARIO · given a visitor opens .roadmap/ on the public repo · when they read the README and example DAG · then they understand the protocol shape via a clean small example, not 350K of internal logs. Validator: total .roadmap/ tracked-file size < 30KB; no leak-blocked terms anywhere in tracked .roadmap/ files.

validate.command: test $(git ls-files .roadmap/ | xargs wc -c 2>/dev/null | tail -1 | awk '{print $1}') -lt 30000

tasks.desc: Audit CLAUDE.md (426 lines) for sibling-project / maintainer-machine references. Read the gitignored sidecar at .roadmap/leak-patterns.local.json for the literal blocklist (codenames + reserved paths) and the per-path allowlist (e.g. 'fleet' is allowed when describing the multi-repo CLI feature, blocked when describing other-project doctrine). Each hit either gets removed or rewritten to a generic example. Preserve: the architecture diagrams, the DAG protocol explanation, the core loop, the tool descriptions, the ML Prague paper reference. Net length should drop modestly. SCENARIO · given a contributor reads CLAUDE.md to understand the project's conventions · when they finish · then they have encountered no sibling-project codename or maintainer-specific absolute path. Validator: scripts/leak-scan.js CLAUDE.md exits 0.

Add an MIT LICENSE file at repo root with exact OSI text, copyright '2026 Ocean Synaptics'. Source the canonical text from https://opensource.org/license/mit (verify text matches the SPDX-listed MIT template; do not paraphrase). SCENARIO · given a license-checker bot scans the repo · when it reads LICENSE · then it identifies MIT cleanly. Validator: file exists, first non-blank line says 'MIT License', text contains the canonical phrases.

validate.command: head -5 LICENSE | grep -q 'MIT License' && grep -q 'Permission is hereby granted, free of charge' LICENSE && grep -q 'WITHOUT WARRANTY OF ANY KIND' LICENSE

tasks.desc: Populate package.json with public-package metadata. Set: name='@ocean-synaptics/roadmap' (unscoped 'roadmap' is taken on npm), license='MIT', description='DAG-governed development protocol — declare typed graphs of work, validate structure, track position via filesystem state', repository={type:'git', url:'git+https://github.com/Ocean-Synaptics/roadmap.git'}, bugs={url:'https://github.com/Ocean-Synaptics/roadmap/issues'}, homepage='https://github.com/Ocean-Synaptics/roadmap#readme', keywords=['dag','workflow','agent','claude-code','typescript','cli','build-tool','governance','ml-prague-2026'], author='Griffin Downs / Ocean Synaptics <griffin.downs@oceansynaptics.com>', files=['dist/','bin/','LICENSE','README.md','CLAUDE.md','templates/'], publishConfig={access:'public'}. Drop husky from devDependencies (already done in s-strip-husky). Update prepare script to drop '&& husky'. Verify scoped name remains available at the moment of running this node (npm view check). SCENARIO · given an npm tooling consumer reads package.json · when they query metadata · then license/repository/bugs/homepage all resolve to public URLs and the name is registry-publishable. Validator: jq queries on each field.

validate.command: jq -e '.name == "@ocean-synaptics/roadmap" and .license == "MIT" and (.description | length) > 20 and .repository.url and .bugs.url and .homepage and (.keywords | length) >= 5 and (.files | length) >= 4 and .publishConfig.access == "public"' package.json
! jq -e '.devDependencies.husky // false' package.json && ! (jq -r '.scripts.prepare' package.json | grep -q husky)

tasks.desc: Rewrite README.md for a public reader. Current README starts with a one-liner intro and an `npm install roadmap` command that points at someone else's package. Required changes: (1) Update install to `pnpm add @ocean-synaptics/roadmap` (or yarn/npm equivalents). (2) Add a status banner at the top explaining the rollout posture (SEE NEXT NODE for exact text). (3) Add a quick-start showing one-DAG-make-orient-advance-terminal cycle on a tiny example. (4) Add a 'Concepts' section listing the four primitives (produces / consumes / depends / validate). (5) Link to the ML Prague paper PDF and the GitHub repo. (6) Add a short 'Origins' section: developed by Ocean Synaptics for managing complex agent-driven workflows; presented as a poster at ML Prague 2026. (7) Drop any reference to ~/.local/share/roadmap clone path (was solo-install instruction). SCENARIO · given a reader who arrives via the ML Prague poster · when they read the top half of the README · then they understand what this tool is, where it came from, how to install it, and that the project welcomes issues. Validator: README contains the new install command, contains 'ML Prague 2026', does NOT contain 'npm install roadmap' (without scope).

validate.command: ! grep -E '^npm install roadmap$' README.md && ! grep -F '~/.local/share/roadmap' README.md

tasks.desc: Add a prominent status banner to README.md right under the title. Exact text (or substantially similar): '> **Status:** Released for ML Prague 2026 (May 2026). The repository is public; **issues are open**; **external PRs are deferred until ~2026-05-15** while the maintainer is at the conference. See [ROLLOUT.md](docs/ROLLOUT.md) for the contribution timeline.' This is the single most important contributor-facing communication; it sets the explicit two-phase expectation that prevents the 'opened a PR, got ghosted' failure mode. SCENARIO · given a new contributor wants to fix something · when they read the README banner · then they file an issue today and (optionally) revisit in 2 weeks for PR acceptance. Validator: banner text contains 'Status', 'ML Prague 2026', 'issues are open', 'PRs are deferred'.

validate.command: head -30 README.md | grep -qi 'status' && head -30 README.md | grep -q 'ML Prague 2026' && head -30 README.md | grep -qi 'issues are open' && head -30 README.md | grep -qi 'PRs.*deferred'

tasks.desc: Author docs/ROLLOUT.md (~1 screen) describing the two-phase rollout plan. Phase 1 (now → ~2026-05-15): public repo, issues on, PRs technically allowed but discouraged via PR template, no Discussions, no formal CONTRIBUTING.md, npm not yet published. Phase 2 (~2026-05-15+): write CONTRIBUTING.md based on conference feedback patterns, enable Discussions, accept PRs, npm publish v0.2.x or v0.3.0. Phase 2 trigger: 'maintainer has triaged the conference issue burst.' Include the v0.2.0 → v0.3.0 trajectory hint. Linked from README banner. SCENARIO · given a contributor curious about the project's posture clicks the banner link · when they read ROLLOUT.md · then they have a clear timeline and know whether to file an issue, wait, or fork. Validator: file exists, contains both phases.

validate.command: grep -qi 'phase 1' docs/ROLLOUT.md && grep -qi 'phase 2' docs/ROLLOUT.md && grep -q 'ML Prague' docs/ROLLOUT.md

tasks.desc: Create .github/ISSUE_TEMPLATE/ with bug.yml + question.yml + config.yml. bug.yml asks for: roadmap version (output of `roadmap --version`), node + pnpm versions, OS, repro steps (minimal), expected vs actual, copy of error output. question.yml asks for: what you tried, what you expected, version. config.yml disables blank issues, links to ROLLOUT.md for the deferred-PR explanation. SCENARIO · given a contributor files an issue · when they pick a template · then the maintainer gets enough context to triage in <2 minutes. Validator: all three files exist, valid YAML.

validate.command: for f in .github/ISSUE_TEMPLATE/*.yml; do node -e "require('fs').readFileSync('$f','utf8').length>0" || exit 1; done

tasks.desc: Create .github/PULL_REQUEST_TEMPLATE.md with explicit deferral text. Exact opening (or close): 'Thanks for opening a PR. **The maintainer is presenting at ML Prague 2026 and is not reviewing external PRs until ~2026-05-15.** If your change is small (typo, README clarification, doc fix), it may still get reviewed. Otherwise, please file an issue first so we can discuss before you write code; this avoids wasted work. After 2026-05-15, this template will be replaced by full PR review guidance.' Soft-fence, not hard reject — the goal is to set expectation, not block contribution. SCENARIO · given a contributor opens a PR · when they see the template · then they understand the timeline and either close-and-file-issue or proceed knowing review is delayed. Validator: file exists, contains key phrases.

validate.command: grep -q 'ML Prague' .github/PULL_REQUEST_TEMPLATE.md && grep -qi '2026-05-15\|May 15' .github/PULL_REQUEST_TEMPLATE.md && grep -qi 'file an issue' .github/PULL_REQUEST_TEMPLATE.md

tasks.desc: Author .github/workflows/test.yml: a GitHub Actions workflow that runs on push to main + on pull_request to main. Steps: checkout (full history for trail.jsonl tests if any), setup-node@v4 (node 22 + 24 matrix — package.json devDeps include @types/node ^25 so target current LTS + bleeding-edge), setup-pnpm, `pnpm install --frozen-lockfile`, `pnpm run check` (typecheck), `pnpm test`. Cache pnpm store. This workflow replaces husky's pre-commit-time gates with PR-time gates, which is the right place for shared-state policy. Add a status badge to README pointing at the workflow. SCENARIO · given a contributor opens a PR · when CI runs · then typecheck + tests pass before merge is allowed. Validator: workflow file is valid YAML, references the right scripts, badge present in README.

validate.command: grep -q 'pnpm run check' .github/workflows/test.yml && grep -q 'pnpm test' .github/workflows/test.yml && grep -qE 'on:\s|on:$|push:|pull_request:' .github/workflows/test.yml

tasks.desc: Set GitHub repository metadata via `gh repo edit Ocean-Synaptics/roadmap`. Fields: --description matching package.json description, --homepage 'https://github.com/Ocean-Synaptics/roadmap', --add-topic dag --add-topic workflow --add-topic agent --add-topic claude-code --add-topic typescript --add-topic cli --add-topic ml-prague-2026 --add-topic governance. License will populate from the LICENSE file automatically once committed (GitHub auto-detects). DO NOT change visibility here — that is the terminal node's job. SCENARIO · given someone searches GitHub for 'dag workflow typescript' · when GitHub indexes this repo · then it surfaces. Validator: gh repo view returns populated description, topics list >= 6, licenseInfo.key == 'mit'.

validate.command: gh repo view Ocean-Synaptics/roadmap --json description,licenseInfo,repositoryTopics | jq -e '(.description | length) > 20 and .licenseInfo.key == "mit" and (.repositoryTopics | length) >= 6'

tasks.desc: Apply branch protection on main matching .github/branch-protections.yml's intent. Concrete via `gh api -X PUT /repos/Ocean-Synaptics/roadmap/branches/main/protection`: require_linear_history true, allow_force_pushes false, allow_deletions false, required_status_checks {strict:true, contexts:['test (22)','test (24)']} once ci-workflow-test runs at least once, enforce_admins true. SOFTEN for phase-1: do NOT yet require pull-request reviews (the maintainer is the only writer; require-PR-review would block self-merges). Update .github/branch-protections.yml to match the actually-applied rules. Add a comment in the YAML pointing to phase-2 ramp (require_code_review true after 2026-05-15). SCENARIO · given the maintainer pushes directly to main without a PR · when GitHub evaluates the push · then it is allowed (single maintainer) but force-pushes and history rewrites are blocked. Validator: gh api confirms required_linear_history.enabled.

validate.command: gh api /repos/Ocean-Synaptics/roadmap/branches/main/protection 2>/dev/null | jq -e '.required_linear_history.enabled == true and .allow_force_pushes.enabled == false'

tasks.desc: Integration gate · simulate a public user's install path end-to-end. Concrete: cp -r the current repo to a tmpdir, cd in, `pnpm install --frozen-lockfile`, `pnpm run build`, `node bin/roadmap.ts --help`, then exercise a tiny DAG: write a 4-node example spec to /tmp, `roadmap make` it, `roadmap orient`, `roadmap advance` each node, terminal. The whole thing must exit 0 on a fresh tree with no maintainer-specific config. SCENARIO · given a contributor following the README install instructions · when they execute every command verbatim · then it works. Falsifier: any non-zero exit, any reference to ~ in output, any hook-prompt blocking the flow.

validate.command: jq -e '.exit_code == 0 and (.steps | length) >= 6 and (.steps | all(.ok == true))' .roadmap/round-2/v-fresh-install.json

tasks.desc: Final pre-flip leak audit. Run scripts/leak-scan.js across the whole tracked tree (`git ls-files`). The scanner reads .roadmap/leak-patterns.local.json (gitignored) for blocked terms, blocked paths, and per-path allowlists, and reports every hit with file:line. Any unallowlisted hit BLOCKS. The scanner exits with non-zero on any hit and writes a structured receipt naming files+lines so the maintainer can either scrub the offender or extend the allowlist (the latter requires explicit edit of the sidecar). SCENARIO · given a security-conscious reader does a basic grep of the public repo for sibling-project codenames · when they run · then they find nothing. Falsifier: any unallowlisted hit. CRITICAL: this validator depends on the sidecar existing locally; if it's missing, the scanner fails closed (exit 1) — public CI won't have the sidecar but public CI also won't run this node, since v-leak-sweep is a maintainer-machine pre-flip gate.

Audit git log for secrets that would be exposed by flipping the repo public. Run: `git log --all -p | grep -nE 'BEGIN [A-Z ]*PRIVATE KEY|api[_-]?key["'\"' ]*[:=]["'\"' ]*[A-Za-z0-9_-]{20,}|secret["'\"' ]*[:=]["'\"' ]*[A-Za-z0-9_-]{16,}|aws_access_key_id|password["'\"' ]*[:=]'`. Any match BLOCKS pending decision (rotate + filter-repo before flip). Note that we explicitly are NOT rewriting history for stylistic concerns (commit messages mentioning fleet/ml-prague are acceptable). This node only blocks on credential-class material. SCENARIO · given a public reader runs `git log -p` looking for bad-secrets-checked-in patterns · when they search · then they find nothing matching. Falsifier: any high-entropy credential pattern.

TERMINAL · the human-meaningful 'done' moment. Two actions, in order: (1) `gh repo edit Ocean-Synaptics/roadmap --visibility public --accept-visibility-change-consequences` — this is irreversible without coordination; PAUSE for human approval before running. (2) `git tag -a v0.2.0 -m 'roadmap v0.2.0 · public release for ML Prague 2026'` then `git push origin v0.2.0`. Verify visibility post-flip via `gh repo view --json visibility`. Verify tag exists on origin via `git ls-remote --tags origin v0.2.0`. SCENARIO · given all upstream verifications pass · when this node runs (after explicit human approval) · then the GitHub repo is publicly visible at https://github.com/Ocean-Synaptics/roadmap and v0.2.0 is the tagged release. The poster's QR code points here. Falsifier: visibility is anything other than 'PUBLIC' or the tag is missing on origin.

validate.command: gh repo view Ocean-Synaptics/roadmap --json visibility | jq -e '.visibility == "PUBLIC"'

tasks.desc: PLAN-MODE · post-conference contribution surface. Cannot be specified concretely now because its inputs (1) the issue burst from the conference, (2) the patterns of confusion in real installs, (3) which features of the README confused readers, (4) what kinds of PRs got submitted during the deferral window — none of these exist yet. After ~2026-05-15, this node decomposes into concrete sub-nodes: author CONTRIBUTING.md (informed by the actual confusion patterns), enable Discussions, replace the deferral PR template with a normal one, raise branch-protection to require code review, npm publish v0.2.x or v0.3.0 (the version bump depends on whether the conference window required hotfixes), update README banner from 'PRs deferred' to 'PRs welcome'. SCENARIO · given the conference is over and ~1 week of issues have been triaged · when this plan node expands · then the resulting children are concrete, falsifiable, and address the actual contributor friction observed. Validator: expanded (children exist with expandedFrom: this node).
